data.title: Strategy buys $264M bitcoin, holdings top 712,000 BTC
data.description:
Strategy bought $264 million in bitcoin, raising its holdings to 712,647 BTC as it continues large scale stock funded crypto accumulation.
source: https://catenaa.com/markets/cryptocurrencies/strategy-buys-264m-bitcoin-holdings-top-712000-btc/

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $19.6449 million yesterday, with BlackRock's IBIT experiencing the largest net outflow at $14.1792 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $19.6449 million yesterday (January 28th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net inflow of $19.451 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $11.434 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a net outflow of $14.1792 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $62.802 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $115.351 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.48%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.334 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $28.0951 million yesterday, with BlackRock's ETHA ETF leading the way with a net inflow of $27.3431 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $28.0951 million yesterday (January 28th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (ETHA) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $27.3431 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $12.454 billion. The Fidelity ETF (FETH) followed with a net inflow of $752,000, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.729 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $18.223 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.01%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $12.383 billion.

data.title: Strive retires most debt and buys 334 Bitcoin
data.description:
Strive cleared 92% of the debt it inherited from Semler, about $110 million.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/strive-retires-most-debt-and-buys-334-btc/

data.title: Yi Lihua: Short sellers overemphasize the four-year cycle; the key is to grasp the main trend of the bull market.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua stated in an article on the X platform that some short sellers are using a "four-year cycle" as their main logic, believing that 2025 has entered a bear market phase similar to 2019-2020, with the market potentially experiencing prolonged sideways movement or even undergoing a shakeout through extreme volatility. He pointed out that Trend Research had previously positioned itself at Ethereum's low point and liquidated its positions during the high-level consolidation phase, and these operations have been verified on-chain. Yi Lihua emphasized that the team will not change its established strategy due to sentiment or short-term noise; the core objective is to grasp the main trend in the long-term cycle of the crypto market, avoid missing bull market opportunities, and thus achieve long-term returns.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Market Trends and Strategy
data.description:
Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua recently shared insights on the X platform regarding market trends. According to Odaily, some bearish investors are using the 'four-year cycle' as a primary logic, suggesting that 2025 might resemble the bear market phase of 2019-2020, with the potential for prolonged sideways trading or extreme volatility.

Yi highlighted that Trend Research strategically positioned itself during Ethereum's low phase and exited during high volatility, with these actions verified on-chain. He emphasized that the team remains committed to its established strategy, focusing on capturing major trends in the crypto market's long cycles to avoid missing bull market opportunities and achieve long-term gains.

data.title: Institutions Show Cautious Approach in Market Positioning
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X that institutions are currently positioning themselves in the market but are not fully committing to aggressive strategies. This cautious approach suggests a wait-and-see attitude as they assess market conditions. The sentiment reflects a broader trend of careful investment strategies amid uncertain economic indicators. Analysts believe this measured positioning could indicate a strategic pause as institutions evaluate potential risks and opportunities in the market.

data.title: Gold's 24-hour market capitalization increase is close to Bitcoin's current total market capitalization.
data.description: According to 8marketcap data, in the past 24 hours, the total market capitalization of gold rose by approximately 4.37%, an increase of about $1.64 trillion; while the current total market capitalization of Bitcoin is approximately $1.74 trillion. The data shows that the daily increase in market capitalization for gold is already close to the current total market capitalization of Bitcoin.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs Experience Significant Outflows Ahead of FOMC Decision
data.description: Santiment posted on X that Bitcoin ETFs have experienced notable outflows as the market anticipates the Federal Open Market Committee's (FOMC) decision today. This trend has been observed since January 15th, indicating a cautious approach by investors in response to potential changes in monetary policy. The movement of funds out of Bitcoin ETFs suggests a shift in market sentiment, reflecting concerns over the economic implications of the FOMC's upcoming announcement.

data.title: AI-Powered Trading Stack Launched by 0G and AmericanFortress
data.description: 0G and AmericanFortress have jointly introduced an AI-native, private, and compliant trading stack. According to Foresight News, this technology merges 0G's computational layer with AmericanFortress's dynamic stealth address technology to address risks such as phishing attacks, address hijacking, and context attacks faced by AI agents in on-chain transactions. The solution supports the generation of encrypted one-time addresses, preventing third parties from linking senders and receivers, thereby ensuring on-chain privacy. Additionally, the technology employs zero-knowledge proofs to conceal balances while maintaining transaction verifiability, allowing for selective disclosure of proofs when necessary to meet compliance requirements. This technology is now live on the 0G mainnet and is being integrated by some institutions, wallets, and Layer 2 ecosystems using SDKs for AI agent frameworks.

data.title: SEC Warns Tokenization Is Not A Workaround For Securities Compliance
data.description:
The SEC says tokenization does not change a stock or bond’s legal status, warning that blockchain-based versions remain fully subject to US securities laws.
source: https://cryptonews.com/news/tokenization-not-workaround-securities-compliance-sec-warns/

data.description:

data.title: Solana Spot ETFs Experience Significant Inflows
data.description:
Solana spot ETFs saw a substantial net inflow of $6.69 million on January 28, Eastern Time. According to Odaily, the Bitwise SOL ETF BSOL led the inflows with $5.01 million in a single day, bringing its historical total net inflow to $690 million. Fidelity SOL ETF FSOL followed with a daily net inflow of $1.11 million, accumulating a historical total net inflow of $152 million.

As of the latest report, the total net asset value of Solana spot ETFs stands at $1.08 billion, with a Solana net asset ratio of 1.52%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $884 million.

data.title: The US Solana spot ETF saw a net inflow of $6.69 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, the Solana spot ETF saw a total net inflow of $6.69 million yesterday (January 28th, Eastern Time). The largest net inflow among spot SOL ETFs yesterday (January 28th, Eastern Time) was into the Bitwise SOL ETF (BSOL), with a single-day net inflow of $5.01 million and a historical total net inflow of $690 million. This was followed by the Fidelity SOL ETF (FSOL), with a single-day net inflow of $1.11 million and a historical total net inflow of $152 million. As of press time, the Solana spot ETF has a total net asset value of $1.08 billion, a net asset value ratio of 1.52%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $884 million.

data.title: Gold and Bitcoin Show Concurrent Rally
data.description: Crypto KOL Ted posted on X about the simultaneous rally of gold and Bitcoin, noting the intriguing nature of this trend. The concurrent rise in value of these two assets has caught the attention of market observers, as both are often seen as safe-haven investments during times of economic uncertainty. Analysts are examining the factors contributing to this parallel movement, considering the potential implications for investors and the broader financial markets. The rally comes amid ongoing discussions about inflation and economic stability, prompting further analysis of how these assets might perform in the near future.

data.title: U.S. Lawmakers Question DOJ's Cryptocurrency Enforcement Decisions
data.description:
U.S. lawmakers have raised concerns over decisions made by Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche regarding the dissolution of the Department of Justice's National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Team (NCET) and the overall reduction in cryptocurrency enforcement efforts. According to PANews, the inquiry follows the disclosure of a letter sent to Blanche, questioning his actions while holding significant cryptocurrency assets.

The letter highlights potential conflicts of interest, referencing Title 18, Section 208(a) of the United States Code. Blanche reportedly disclosed ownership of cryptocurrencies valued between $158,000 and $470,000, primarily Bitcoin and Ethereum, in January 2025. In April of the same year, he issued a memorandum to reduce DOJ's cryptocurrency enforcement activities, with his assets being disposed of only by late May to early June.

Lawmakers have requested an explanation for Blanche's lack of recusal during the decision-making process and a detailed account of the DOJ's previous claims that the matter was "appropriately flagged, handled, and approved." The letter demands that Blanche provide communication records, ethics review documents, and responses to seven specific questions by February 11, 2026.

The legislators emphasized the importance of enforcing laws against crimes involving cryptocurrencies, asserting that this inquiry fulfills Congress's oversight responsibilities regarding DOJ personnel and policy decisions.

data.title: Hong Kong's First Gold ETF Sees Strong Debut
data.description: Hong Kong's first gold ETF, issued by Hang Seng, debuted with a notable increase in value, trading at 17.41 HKD, marking an 8.81% rise. According to PANews, the Hang Seng Gold ETF holds physical gold, with all bars stored in designated vaults in Hong Kong. The ETF plans to introduce tokenized non-listed fund units, with HSBC serving as the tokenization agent. Initially, Ethereum will be used as the primary blockchain, with potential expansion to other public blockchains offering similar security and distributed ledger technology. Fund unit holders can subscribe or redeem tokenized units through qualified distributors, as these tokenized shares are not available for secondary market trading.

data.title: US senators accuse the deputy attorney general of shutting down the Justice Department’s crypto enforcement team due to his possession of crypto assets.
data.description: On January 28, six U.S. senators, including Mazie K. Hirono, Elizabeth Warren, and Richard Durbin, sent a letter to Deputy Attorney General Todd Blanche questioning his decision to disband the Department of Justice's National Cryptocurrency Enforcement Task Force (NCET) in April 2025. The senators pointed out that Blanche held approximately $158,000 to $470,000 worth of cryptocurrency at the time of the decision, potentially constituting a conflict of interest and violating federal laws regarding the influence of personal financial interests on official decisions. The letter stated that shutting down the enforcement task force was considered a "serious mistake" and could facilitate sanctions evasion, drug trafficking, and fraud. The senators cited data indicating that illicit cryptocurrency activity increased by 162% year-over-year in 2025, primarily driven by inflows of funds related to sanctioned entities and money laundering networks. Blanche is currently the subject of a complaint filed by the Department of Justice's Office of the Inspector General.
